pattern: A greedy left-to-right decode where a leading 1 always consumes two bits decides whether the final zero stands alone.
hints: Decoding is forced: a 0 is a one-bit character, while a 1 always begins a two-bit character.
Scan from the left, advancing by two whenever you see a 1 and by one whenever you see a 0.
The last character is a one-bit character exactly when your scan lands on the final index, i.e. the trailing 0 is reached on its own.

edge_inputs.description: lone zero is a one-bit character
single two-bit character then a final zero
leading zeros then a final lone zero
many two-bit characters before a lone final zero
the final zero is the tail of a two-bit character

stress.description: valid random sequences built from one-bit and two-bit characters, always ending in 0
stress.note: guarantees the array ends with 0 as required
